expectations.text: States neither modality is universally superior for hypertrophy
expectations.evidence: States: 'Neither is universally better—both drive hypertrophy effectively.'

expectations.text: Notes machines are useful for variation, reducing technical constraints, and targeting volume
expectations.evidence: Discusses isolation, joint safety, consistency, and metabolic stress advantages of machines.

expectations.text: Recommends keeping a base of compound free-weight work
expectations.evidence: States: 'Build your program around compound free-weight movements.'

expectations.text: Practical takeaway: use both, pick the tool that best fits the muscle/movement/fatigue profile
expectations.evidence: States: 'A well-designed program uses both, with free weights as the foundation and machines as specialized tools.'
